---
source: "The Conversation Africa"
author: "Mark Ireland, Senior Lecturer in Energy Geoscience, Newcastle University"
title: >
  What the UK’s first geothermal power plant means for the nation’s electricity supply
description: >
  Unlike wind and solar which are weather-dependent sources of renewable energy, geothermal heat can produce energy steadily, day and night, all year round.
url: "https://theconversation.com/what-the-uks-first-geothermal-power-plant-means-for-the-nations-electricity-supply-276909"
image: "https://images.theconversation.com/files/720657/original/file-20260226-57-h8vmro.jpg?ixlib=rb-4.1.0&rect=0%2C504%2C4032%2C2016&q=45&auto=format&w=1356&h=668&fit=crop"
published_at: 2026-02-27
content: >
  More than half of the UK’s electrical power was supplied by renewable energy on February 25 2026.   That consisted mainly of solar, wind and hydroelectric sources. The next day, a new source of elect… [+5745 chars]
terms: "Cost of electricity"
search_timestamp: 2026-03-01 02:07:05
---


Published: {{< meta published_at >}}


Source: {{< meta source >}}


[Read full article]({{< meta url >}}){target='_blank'}



![](`r rmarkdown::metadata$image[1]`)
